title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
input_row.rationale: Kubelets, by default, allow all authenticated requests (even anonymous ones) without needing explicit authorization checks from the apiserver. You should restrict this behavior and only allow explicitly authorized requests.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift 4, the Kubernetes configuration file is managed by the Machine Config Operator. By default, OpenShift rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized users. You can verify that each node in the cluster is configured to only accept authenticated users with the following command: for node in $(oc get nodes -ojsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/$node/proxy/configz | jq '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode' done Verify none of the nodes return AlwaysAllow for the authorization mode.
input_row.default_value: By default, OpenShift uses Webhook authorization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control regarding the `--authorization-mode` not being set to `AlwaysAllow` can be effectively automated. The provided audit command leverages the `oc` command-line tool to check the configuration of nodes in a Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster. Given that OpenShift is part of IBM Cloud services, and existing APIs will allow verification of this control.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_node_authorization_mode_not_always_allow

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use IBM Cloud CLI commands to query node configurations in an OpenShift cluster.
- Execute the command to list all nodes: `oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'`.
- Iterate through each node's kubelet configuration using the API endpoint: `oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/<node_name>/proxy/configz`.
- Inspect the `authorization.mode` property to ensure it does not return `AlwaysAllow`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** None of the nodes return `AlwaysAllow` for the `authorization.mode`.
  - **Fail:** At least one node returns `AlwaysAllow`.

Example of the command for each node:
```bash
for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do 
  oc get --raw /api/v1/nodes/$node/proxy/configz | jq '.kubeletconfig.authorization.mode' 
done
```
This command checks the authorization mode for each node and alerts if any are incorrectly configured to accept all requests.
